title: rubus micans subsp. subrotundus
intro: Rubus micans subsp. subrotundus, also known as the 'Muscovite' or 'Baltic Bramble', is a subspecies of the Rubus micans species, a type of wild raspberry native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and inflammation, due to its rich content of anthocyanins and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Rubus micans subsp. subrotundus include reducing oxidative stress, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting antimicrobial and anti-cancer properties. Traditionally, the plant is prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, allowing its medicinal compounds to be absorbed and utilized by the body.
